label: Calculus & automatic differentiation theory

node.label: Calculus & automatic differentiation theory
node.definition: Derivatives, gradients, Jacobians, chain rules, and automatic differentiation explain how parameter changes affect objectives and computation graphs.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory is modeled as an upstream prerequisite for downstream nodes such as Optimization theory, Numerical analysis, Differential equations.
examples: atlas downstream link to Optimization theory
atlas downstream link to Numerical analysis
atlas downstream link to Differential equations
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting.
